headline: Apple builds core AI architecture around Google Gemini models
body: Apple has revealed a new AI architecture centered on Google's Gemini models, signaling major vendor consolidation in enterprise AI infrastructure. This partnership carries immediate implications for healthcare organizations evaluating AI platforms: it narrows the competitive landscape and creates potential lock-in risks around Gemini's performance, pricing stability, and clinical validation. Healthcare systems must monitor Gemini's reliability issues (including recent caching bugs causing $1K/hour charges) and clarify how Apple's endorsement affects their own AI strategy, particularly for EHR integration and clinical decision support. For healthcare AI consultants, this move underscores the need to pressure vendors on transparent pricing, SLA commitments, and compliance certifications before widespread clinical deployment.
